title: LLM Field Name Mapping Response
description: Proposed mappings from source columns onto the PCOS canonical schema. One entry per input column. The model maps columns to fields and infers source units; it does not map data values, convert values, or compute derived fields.

mappings.description: One entry per source column in the input. Every column must appear exactly once, including unmappable ones.

column_id.description: Id of the source column from the input.

field_id.description: Canonical field id from the catalog. Null when no field applies - prefer null over a low-confidence guess.

confidence.description: Confidence that this column corresponds to this field.

unit_raw.description: Source unit, stated or inferred from the value range. Null if it cannot be determined - never guess. Omit for non-numeric fields.

unit_confidence.description: Confidence in unit_raw, separate from mapping confidence. A column may be confidently identified while its unit stays ambiguous.

flags.description: Conditions requiring human attention before this mapping is used.
